2008-08-27.abstract: Foreign maids in Lebanon have been dying at a rate of more than one a week, often through suicide or failed attempts to escape from abusive employers, a report said.

2008-08-27.title: Britain: Suspect Arrested in Threats to Prime Minister
2008-08-27.abstract: A 25-year-old terrorism suspect was arrested in Blackburn, Lancashire in connection with terrorist threats made against Prime Minister Gordon Brown and former Prime Minister Tony Blair.

2008-08-27.title: Taliban Gain New Foothold in Afghan City
2008-08-27.abstract: A June jailbreak highlighted the Afghan government’s frailty, and the security situation has worsened since.

2008-08-28.title: Israel, After 43 Years, Is Ready for Beatlemania
2008-08-28.abstract: Excitement is building for the first concert by Paul McCartney in Israel, an epilogue to a tale that began in 1965 when authorities canceled a Beatles concert on “spiritual and cultural” grounds.

2008-08-28.title: U.S. Officials Describe Afghan Airstrike
2008-08-28.abstract: American military officials sought to refute claims that as many as 90 civilians, most of them children, were killed in airstrikes on a village in western Afghanistan last week.

2008-08-28.title: The Hague: Former Employee Is Accused of Contempt of Court
2008-08-28.abstract: Florence Hartmann, a former spokeswoman for the prosecution at the United Nations war crimes tribunal, is accused to have disclosed confidential information in a book after she left the court.

2008-08-28.title: Zimbabwe: No Power-Sharing as Mugabe Forms Government
2008-08-28.abstract: President Robert Mugabe said he planned to appoint cabinet ministers and form a government unilaterally, the state-owned newspaper reported.

2008-08-28.title: Sarkozy Reaches Out to Syria
2008-08-28.abstract: President Nicolas Sarkozy of France has announced plans to go to Syria next Wednesday to pursue restoring diplomatic ties.

2008-08-29.title: An Embattled Enclave Yearns to Be Free (and Liechtenstein)
2008-08-29.abstract: Russia’s recognition of South Ossetia and Abkhazia as independent nations has filled people in the region with hope that other countries will follow.

2008-08-29.title: A Hard Habit to Break, Even With Gas at $10 a Gallon
2008-08-29.abstract: Transportation experts say that many Europeans, out of necessity, habit or love, have proved surprisingly willing to bear the extra cost of driving.

2008-08-29.title: China Defends Its Reviled Soccer Team
2008-08-29.abstract: The government is taking a step to shore up the reputation of that most dubious of national sports icons: the men’s soccer team.

2008-08-29.title: Abie Nathan, Israeli Peace Champion, Dies at 81
2008-08-29.abstract: Mr. Nathan is best remembered for his quirky quest for peace in a 30-year campaign waged through a series of audacious escapades by land, air and sea.

2008-08-29.title: Ex-Marine Is Cleared in Killing of Unarmed Iraqi Detainees
2008-08-29.abstract: The trial was the first in which a civilian jury had determined whether a former military service member had violated the law of war in combat.

2008-08-30.title: Joint Inquiry on Deaths of Afghans Is Proposed
2008-08-30.abstract: The American commander of the NATO force in Afghanistan offered to conduct a joint investigation on the loss of civilian life in the airstrikes from last week.

2008-08-30.title: Britain: U.S. Acts on Court Files
2008-08-30.abstract: In a reversal, the Bush administration turned over documents that may support allegations by a Guantánamo Bay detainee that he was tortured while in American custody in Pakistan.

2008-08-30.title: Crusader Sees Wealth as Cure for Caste Bias
2008-08-30.abstract: Chandra Bhan Prasad’s argues that India’s economic liberalization is about to destroy the caste system.

2008-08-30.title: Lebanon: Suspect Handed Over
2008-08-30.abstract: Hezbollah on Friday handed over a gunman suspected of firing on a Lebanese Army helicopter a day earlier, killing its pilot, army officials said. The Shiite group said in a statement that the killing was “very unfortunate and painful,” but it did not say if its members were involved. The pilot, First Lt. Samer Hanna, died when his helicopter came under fire in southern Lebanon over an area that Hezbollah controls. Hundreds of people attended his funeral on Friday, and his death has become a rallying point for calls for Hezbollah to relinquish its weapons.

2008-08-30.title: The Dalits of India
2008-08-30.abstract: On a journey across villages in India, it is difficult to square the utter destitution of the untouchables with their empowerment.

2008-08-31.title: Cease-Fire by Pakistan in Attacks on Militants
2008-08-31.abstract: An end to air assaults by the Pakistani military has raised concerns about progress in a tribal region where Al Qaeda and Taliban forces have forged ties.

2008-08-31.title: Toll in China Quake Is Worse Than Feared
2008-08-31.abstract: A 6.1-magnitude earthquake that struck Sichuan Province Saturday destroyed more than 100,000 homes and caused at least 28 deaths, China’s state-run media reported.

2008-08-31.title: Thai Government Backers Take to Streets
2008-08-31.abstract: The action was intended to counter protesters who have been demonstrating against the government for a week.
